top_signals.title: Australia spy chief warns of rising threat of Iran-backed terrorism
top_signals.summary: ASIO’s Mike Burgess says war in Middle East could fuel further violence by Tehran-aligned groups abroad
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Rheinmetall Sinks as Germany Shelves Major Warship Contract
top_signals.summary: Rheinmetall AG shares plunged Wednesday as Germany withdrew from a key warships contract, dealing a blow to investor confidence in the defense contractor following years of huge gains.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Tech companies would have to pay AI data center energy costs under bill moving in Congress
top_signals.summary: A House subcommittee may advance legislation Wednesday to make tech companies pay the energy costs for operating data centers to power AI.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Alan Greenspan, Influential Fed Chairman Whose Legacy Was Dimmed by the Financial Crisis, Dies at 100 - WSJ
top_signals.summary: Alan Greenspan, Influential Fed Chairman Whose Legacy Was Dimmed by the Financial Crisis, Dies at 100  WSJ
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
top_signals.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
